figures.caption: Figure 5. VideoPhy Physical Commonsense (PC) score by category.
Figure 8. VLM instruction to generate the physics caption for a video chunk
Figure 14. Supplementary qualitative results from the ablation study. All prompts are from VideoPhy.
Figure 6. VideoPhy2 Physical Commonsense (PC) score by category.

Table 2. Quantitative comparisons of our approach with previous works evaluated on the VideoPhy benchmark, including both general and physics-aware generative video methods. Symbols indicate results taken from prior work: WISA [49] (†),VideoREPA [66] ($), Hao et al. [20] (#),PhyT2V [56] (∼),VideoPhy [3] (‡). Our own baselines are marked with ∗. Best model scores are underlined. For physics-aware methods with multiple entries, we report the best PC score, with parentheses showing relative improvement over the corresponding baseline.
Figure 2. Procedure for generating physics-grounded local prompts during data annotation.

Figure 11. A sample from the data with annotations generated by a Vision Language Model (VLM). The topmost text is the global prompt and the local annotations are listed sequentially alongside a representative frame from each chunk.
Table 3. Ablations. The highest scores in each metric are highlighted, whereas the lowest scores are underlined.
Figure 7. Qualitative ablation example on a VideoPhy prompt. Best viewed when zoomed in.

Figure 3. Architecture of PhysVid showing local information pathways with chunk aware cross-attention. Commonly applied procedures such as tokenization, latent encoding, and decoding are implicit and not shown.
Figure 12. A set of physics grounded and physics counterfactual prompts generated during inference. The representative frames from the generated video chunks are shown on the right.
Figure 10. VLM instruction to generate the local physics captions for all the chunks to be generated at once during inference
Figure 13. Additional comparisons between PhysVid and Wan-14B. Captions are from VideoPhy.
Figure 4. Generation of local and counterfactual prompts during inference.
Figure 9. VLM instruction to generate the counterfactual physics caption
